steps.title: Calculate the slope from the two data points
steps.content:
Use the slope formula with the given points $(1, 155)$ and $(3, 285)$:
$$m = \frac{f(3) - f(1)}{3 - 1} = \frac{285 - 155}{3 - 1} = \frac{130}{2} = 65$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept using one data point
steps.content:
Substitute the point $(1, 155)$ and slope $m = 65$ into $f(h) = mh + b$:
$$155 = 65(1) + b$$
$$155 = 65 + b$$
$$b = 90$$
The equation is $f(h) = 65h + 90$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
